nodes.name: 掘穴鸚鵡
nodes.summary: 掘穴鸚鵡（Burrowing parrot，学名 Cyanoliseus patagonus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛、稀树草原，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The burrowing parrot (Cyanoliseus patagonus), also known as the burrowing parakeet or the Patagonian conure, is a species of parrot native to Argentina and Chile

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The burrowing parrot (Cyanoliseus patagonus), also known as the burrowing parakeet or the Patagonian conure, is a species of parrot native to Argentina and Chile

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The burrowing parrot can be found in much of Argentina, and there are isolated populations in central Chile. In the winter, birds in central and southern Argentina may migrate north as far as southern Uruguay, making them austral migrants, while Chilean birds migrate vertically down slope to avoid colder altitudes. Movements in the populations of northwestern Argentina are also known to occur according to food availability. The burrowing parrot prefers dry, open country, particularly in the vicinity of water courses, up to 2000 m in elevation. Habitats include montane grassy shrubland, Patagonian steppes, arid lowland, woodland savanna, and the plains of the Gran Chaco. They may also inhabit farmland and the edges of urban areas

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|251x251px|Burrowing parrot in captivity The burrowing parrot currently has an overall conservation status of least concern according to the IUCN Red List, but populations are currently declining, due to exploitation for the wildlife trade and persecution as a crop pest. Their nesting habits make them particularly vulnerable to human disturbances and habitat degradation.
